title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. dufftii
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. dufftii, also known as the Dufft's orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its saponin and phenolic content. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. dufftii include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. dufftii can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
